items.title: fix(container): update image ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw (2026.3.24 → 2026.3.28)
items.content:
This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|
| [ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw](https://openclaw.ai) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw)) | patch | `2026.3.24` → `2026.3.28` |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>openclaw/openclaw (ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw)</summary>

### [`v2026.3.28`](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2026328)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/compare/v2026.3.24...v2026.3.28)

##### Breaking

- Providers/Qwen: remove the deprecated `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration for `portal.qwen.ai`; migrate to Model Studio with `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key`. ([#​52709](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/52709)) Thanks [@​pomelo-nwu](https://redirect.github.com/pomelo-nwu).
- Config/Doctor: drop automatic config migrations older than two months; very old legacy keys now fail validation instead of being rewritten on load or by `openclaw doctor`.

##### Changes

- xAI/tools: move the bundled xAI provider to the Responses API, add first-class `x_search`, and auto-enable the xAI plugin from owned web-search and tool config so bundled Grok auth/configured search flows work without manual plugin toggles. ([#​56048](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56048)) Thanks [@​huntharo](https://redirect.github.com/huntharo).
- xAI/onboarding: let the bundled Grok web-search plugin offer optional `x_search` setup during `openclaw onboard` and `openclaw configure --section web`, including an x\_search model picker with the shared xAI key.
- MiniMax: add image generation provider for `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control. ([#​54487](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/54487)) Thanks [@​liyuan97](https://redirect.github.com/liyuan97).
- Plugins/hooks: add async `requireApproval` to `before

## Summary by cubic
Moved MCP Server docs to Integrations and added a collapsed Legacy (v2) section with a single `Agent (v2)` page that consolidates v2 models, files, streaming, and parameters. Simplified Quickstart and cleaned up the navbar and redirects for faster navigation.

- **Refactors**
  - Moved MCP Server to Integrations; removed its icon; removed Playwright from Integrations.
  - Added Legacy (v2) under Concepts with `Agent (v2)` and `Skills`; extracted from Tasks; set a robot icon on `Agent (v2)`.
  - Marked Browser Use 2.0 as the default in the v2 models table.
  - Removed pricing/models pages; added redirects: `/cloud/pricing` and `/cloud/models` → `/cloud/legacy/agent`, `/cloud/guides/skills` → `/cloud/legacy/skills`.
  - Navbar: text labels for GitHub/Discord, removed Status; primary button now “Dashboard”.
  - Quickstart trimmed to install + run.

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-03-29)

> **数据概览**: 过去 24 小时内，项目共有 **500 条 Issues** 更新（活跃度极高）与 **500 条 PRs** 更新，发布了 **2 个** 版本。项目正处于高活跃开发周期，主要聚焦于多通道稳定性和平台适配。

---

### 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 社区今日展现了极高的活跃度，单日 Issue 活跃数达到 500 条，反映出用户群体的快速扩张与使用场景的复杂化。核心修复集中在 **通信渠道的稳定性**（Discord、Telegram、LINE）和 **平台兼容性回归**（Windows/Linux、Docker），特别是针对最近版本（v2026.3.2x）引入的连接中断和认证失效问题进行了紧急修补。功能方面，社区正在热烈讨论原生 Windows/Linux 客户端的支持，同时代码库正大规模推进**子代理并行编排**与**插件化运行时**的重构，预示着架构层面的一次重要升级。

---

### 2. 版本发布
今日发布了 **v2026.3.28** 正式版及 **v2026.3.28-beta.1**，主要包含以下破坏性变更与修复：

-   **Breaking: Qwen 提供商迁移**
    -   移除了已废弃的 `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth 集成。
    -   **迁移指引**: 用户需使用 `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key` 迁移至 Model Studio API Key 认证方式。[详情 #52709](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/52709)
-   **Config/Doctor 清理**
    -   移除了对两个月以前旧版配置的自动迁移支持，建议长期用户确保配置文件为最新格式。

---

### 3. 项目进展
今日合并或处于活跃状态的重要 PR 推动了以下关键领域的进展：

-   **内存系统革新**: [PR #54750](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/54750) 引入了受神经科学启发的 **情节记忆系统**，为 Agent 增加了类似海马体的长期记忆能力，补齐了生物记忆系统的最后一块拼图。
-   **子代理编排增强**: [PR #56673](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56673) 提议新增 `sessions_await` 工具，解

items.title: fix: audit bugfixes — P1+P2 from investigate report
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes from post-implementation investigate report (13 findings, 4 P1, 7 P2).

### P1 Fixes
- **Summary dedup (F-0-11)**: Verified — NOT EXISTS check already correct in Task 19 SQL
- **OpenClaw before_tool_call (F-0-2)**: Added `BeforeToolCallResult` to `HookResult` type so file context injection works

### P2 Fixes
- **Store content validation (F-0-1)**: Error message clarified for store_memory without content
- **Summary threshold (F-0-3)**: Lowered userPrompt fallback from 50→10 chars — short prompts now generate summaries
- **Concept backfill (F-0-9)**: Migration 064 adds keywords for 5 missing concepts (why-it-exists, what-changed, anti-pattern, gotcha, trade-off)

### Already Fixed in Prior PRs
- **Circuit breaker logging (F-0-5)**: Fixed in PR #124

## Test plan
- [ ] `go build ./...` passes
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes for openclaw-engram
- [ ] After deploy: all 20 semantic concepts have data
- [ ] Short prompts generate summaries (threshold 10 chars)

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Исправления ошибок**
  * Уточнено сообщение об ошибке при сохранении памяти.
  * Смягчена минимальная длина входа для генерации сводки (более гибкая валидация).

* **Новые возможности**
  * Добавлен хук-событие перед вызовом инструментов для расширения плагинов.

* **Обслуживание данных**
  * Добавлена миграция для автозаполнения недостающих концептов в записях наблюдений.

OpenClaw 社区在 2026-03-29 继续保持高活跃度，过去 24 小时内共处理 **500 条 Issues**（新开/活跃 442 条，关闭 58 条）和 **500 条 PRs**（待合并 349 条，已合并/关闭 151 条），显示出强劲的开发与问题响应节奏。项目发布了一个新版本 `v2026.3.28-beta.1`，包含关键性破坏变更和配置迁移要求。当前社区焦点集中在 Discord 连接稳定性、多平台支持缺失、认证机制回归等问题上，反映出用户对生产环境可靠性的高度关注。

---

## 2. 版本发布

### 🔴 [v2026.3.28-beta.1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.3.28-beta.1)（2026-03-28）

**破坏性变更（Breaking Changes）：**
- **移除 Qwen Portal OAuth 集成**：废弃 `qwen-portal-auth` 认证方式（原用于 `portal.qwen.ai`），强制迁移至 Model Studio 的 API Key 认证。用户需通过 `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key` 完成迁移（#52709）。
- **停止自动配置迁移**：`Config/Doctor` 模块不再自动迁移旧版配置，用户需手动检查并更新 `openclaw.json` 文件以避免启动失败。

> ⚠️ **迁移建议**：所有使用 Qwen 模型的用户应立即执行 onboard 流程更新认证方式；升级前建议备份配置文件并运行 `openclaw doctor` 检查兼容性。

---

## 3. 项目进展

今日有 **151 个 PR 被合并或关闭**，重点进展包括：

- **Discord 稳定性修复**：多个高优先级 PR 解决了 WebSocket 断连导致的网关崩溃问题（#56164、#54270），通过抑制持久监听器错误和修复 botUserId 作用域问题，显著提升服务可用性。
- **Mistral API 兼容性修复**：#56667 将 Mistral 提供者从 `openai-completions` 切换至原生 `mistral-conversations` API，彻底解决长期存在的 422 错误（#47079、#53363）。
- **子代理功能增强**：#56673 引入 `sessions_await` 工具，支持并行子代理协调；#56528 增加结构化输出支持，为复杂工作流打下基础。
- **配置系统改进**：#56190 支持 `${VAR:-default}` 环境变量回退语法，提升部署灵活性。

整体来看，项目在**通信稳

## Bellink MCP Server

**Name:** Bellink  
**Website:** https://www.bellink.io  
**npm:** [bellink-mcp](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bellink-mcp)  
**GitHub:** https://github.com/barflotek/bellink-mcp  
**Transport:** SSE (hosted) + stdio (npm package)  
**Category:** Business Tools / Integration Gateway

### Description

Bellink connects AI assistants to 30+ business tools through one MCP server. Users sign up, connect their apps via OAuth, and get an MCP endpoint that works with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Perplexity, and OpenClaw.

### Supported Apps

Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Sheets, Google Docs, Google Drive, Google Analytics, Google Forms, Mindbody, Meta (Facebook + Instagram + Ads), Linear, Airtable, Notion, Stripe, Slack, GitHub, Figma, Allegro, QuickBooks, Twilio, Webflow, WordPress, Typeform, Calendly, Beehiiv, Buttondown, n8n, Zoom, Shopify, WhatsApp, Outlook

### Pricing

- Starter: Free forever — all apps (except Mindbody), 250 requests/month
- Personal: $29/mo — all apps including Mindbody, 1,000 requests/month  
- Business: $99/mo — all apps, 10K requests/month, 5 seats
items.author: barflotek

items.title: Generic architecture for browser-bound MCP tools over Tailscale trust networks
items.content:
## Summary

Today, browser-bound MCP integrations tend to fall into two extremes:

1. **Local-only browser bridges**  
   - browser extension / localhost-only companion
   - great for single-machine use
   - hard to reuse from other trusted devices

2. **Raw browser automation / DevTools exposure**  
   - powerful, but too low-level and too risky to expose broadly
   - poor security boundary if shared across a trust network

There is a useful middle ground that OpenClaw could support/document as a **generic architecture pattern**:

- keep the browser/session runtime local to one trusted host
- expose only high-level MCP tools to the rest of the trusted network
- use Tailscale (or equivalent private network) as the transport boundary

This would enable “browser capability as a private infrastructure service” without exposing raw browser control.

---

## Problem

For integrations that depend on:

- a real logged-in browser session
- cookies / localStorage / browser fingerprint
- extension-based site access
- site-specific flows (e.g. X, XiaoHongShu, LinkedIn, etc.)

`localhost` is often too limiting, but exposing raw CDP / DevTools / extension internals is not desirable.

What users often want instead is:

- one persistent **browser host**
- accessible from multiple trusted devices / agents
- over a private trust network such as **Tailscale**
- while only exposing constrained, auditable, high-level capabilities

Examples:

- `search_x_posts(query)`
- `read_x_post(url)`
- `search_xhs(keyword, filters)`
- `get_xhs_feed_detail(feed_id, xsec_token)`

Not:

- arbitrary JS eval
- full CDP control
- unrestricted browser navigation
- cookie export

---

## Proposed Architecture

### 1. Browser Host Runtime
A fixed machine runs:

- Chrome/Edge
- logged-in user profile
- browser extensions / content scripts as needed
- real session state (cookies, storage, fingerprint, etc.)

This host is the only place that directly touches the website runtime.

### 2. Local Bridge
A localhos

items.title: feat: implement zao-api MCP server for OpenClaw agents
items.content:
## Summary

Implements Phase 3 of the memory/knowledge graph vision. Closes issue #14.

## What was built

### MCP Server (`src/mcp/zao-api/`)

All 8 tools implemented with proper TypeScript types and MCP schemas:

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `get_profile` | User profile by FID |
| `get_casts` | Recent casts for a user |
| `get_respect_score` | Respect score, rank, weekly delta |
| `get_community_members` | Active community members |
| `get_recents` | Recent casts across community |
| `search_casts` | Search casts by keyword |
| `get_room_history` | Recent audio rooms |
| `get_proposals` | Governance proposals |

### Implementation Details

- **Transport:** `StdioServerTransport` — stdio-based for OpenClaw MCP compatibility
- **Supabase:** Service role client (bypasses RLS, server-side only)
- **Security:** All queries parameterized (no SQL injection)
- **Errors:** `MCPError` class with `{ error, code }` format
- **Timeouts:** 10-second timeout on all Supabase queries via `Promise.race`
- **Build:** Compiles to `dist/index.js` with `npm run build`

### Files

```
src/mcp/zao-api/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── src/
    ├── index.ts
    ├── lib/
    │   ├── supabase.ts
    │   └── errors.ts
    ├── tools/
    │   ├── index.ts (8 tools)
    │   └── [8 individual tool files]
    └── types/
        └── index.ts
```

### OpenClaw Integration

Add to OpenClaw config:
```json
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "mcp": {
        "servers": {
          "zao-api": {
            "command": "node",
            "args": ["/path/to/dist/index.js"]
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Closes #14

items.title: feat: implement Hindsight memory system for ZAO OS agents
items.content:
## Summary

Implements Phase 2 of the memory/knowledge graph vision. Closes issue #13.

## What was built

### Infrastructure
- `infra/hindsight/docker-compose.yml` — Hindsight server + PostgreSQL 15 stack
- `infra/hindsight/.env.example` — Environment template

### Core Libraries
- `src/lib/hindsight.ts` — TypeScript client singleton
- `src/lib/memory-events.ts` — Event triggers (onCastReceived, onTrackShared, onRespectTransaction, onRoomEvent, onProfileUpdate)
- `src/lib/memory-recall.ts` — Recall/reflect helpers + prompt injection for AI context

### API Routes
- `POST /api/memory/{userId}/retain` — Store a memory
- `GET /api/memory/{userId}/recall` — Retrieve relevant memories
- `POST /api/memory/{userId}/reflect` — Generate taste insights
- `GET /api/memory/community/recall` — Community knowledge
- `POST /api/cron/weekly-reflection` — Weekly taste synthesis cron (Sunday 18:00 UTC)

### MCP Server
- `mcp/hindsight-mcp-server/` — OpenClaw agent wrapper exposing tools: `memory_retain`, `memory_recall`, `memory_reflect`

### Database
- `supabase/migrations/20260329000000_create_memory_tables.sql` — taste_profiles + memory_events schema with RLS

## Key Design Decisions

- Graceful degradation if Hindsight service is unavailable
- FID used as bank_id for per-user memory isolation
- Weekly reflection scheduled for Sunday 18:00 UTC
- MCP server is stdio-based for OpenClaw compatibility

Closes #13

items.title: fix(task-store): tolerate Windows EPERM/EACCES rename during atomic save
items.content:
fix(task-store): tolerate intermittent Windows EPERM/EACCES rename failures when saving tasks

---

## Summary

On Windows (especially in corporate environments with antivirus / file indexers / DLP), atomic `rename(tmp -> target)` can intermittently fail with `EPERM`/`EACCES`. When this happens inside the A2A gateway task store, the durable task state cannot be persisted and long-running A2A flows (async task mode, `--non-blocking --wait` / `tasks/get`) become unreliable.

This PR makes `FileTaskStore.save()` more resilient:
- keep atomic rename as the fast/primary path
- when rename fails with `EPERM`/`EACCES`, retry a few times with small backoff
- if it still fails, fall back to a best-effort non-atomic overwrite write, and clean up the tmp file

This changes a hard failure into a recoverable path so polling can continue.

---

## Why this matters

A2A async task mode relies on being able to persist incremental task states. A single failure to write the task JSON can break polling and make agent-to-agent messaging appear flaky.

---

## Repro (Windows)

1) Run A2A Gateway on Windows.
2) Trigger async tasks at a moderate rate (or concurrently), especially on a machine with active antivirus/indexing.
3) This is easiest to reproduce when running multiple OpenClaw profiles on the same machine/user (shared task store directory), but the underlying file-lock race can also happen in single-gateway setups.
4) Observe intermittent:

```
EPERM: operation not permitted, rename '<task>.json.<pid>.<ts>.tmp' -> '<task>.json'
```

---

## Fix

`src/task-store.ts`:
- wrap `rename(tmp, target)` in a try/catch
- only handle `EPERM`/`EACCES`
- short backoff retries
- fallback to `writeFile(target, payload)` + best-effort tmp cleanup

---

## Deployment / config note (recommended)

When running multiple OpenClaw profiles on the same Windows user, do not share the default task store directory.

Recommend configuring per-profile:
- `p

items.title: Silent channel disable when plugins.allow excludes bundled channel plugins
items.content:
## Bug Report

**Version:** 2026.3.24 / 2026.3.28-beta.1
**Platform:** macOS arm64, Node 25.8.0

## Summary

When `plugins.allow` is set but does not include `telegram` or `whatsapp`, these channel plugins are silently disabled. The gateway boots normally, `openclaw doctor` passes, `config validate` passes, and `openclaw status` shows `gateway=loaded` — but channels never initialize. The channels list is simply empty with no explanation.

## Root Cause

`resolveEffectiveEnableState()` in `config-state-BCw27kbO.js` returns `{ enabled: false, reason: 'not in allowlist' }` for channel plugins when they're missing from `plugins.allow`. This happens silently — no log warning, no status indication, no doctor alert.

Interestingly, `resolveGatewayStartupPluginIds()` correctly identifies telegram/whatsapp as needed based on channel config, but `resolveEffectiveEnableState()` overrides this without any output.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have a working OpenClaw setup with Telegram and WhatsApp configured
2. Install a third-party plugin that modifies `plugins.allow`
3. While debugging the third-party plugin, reduce `plugins.allow` to only the third-party plugin ID (e.g., `['acpx']`)
4. Restart the gateway
5. Channels silently disappear — no error, no warning

## Impact

This caused a 2.5-hour outage. Every diagnostic tool said the system was healthy. The root cause was only found by reading the minified dist source and calling `resolveEffectiveEnableState()` directly in a REPL.

## Expected Behavior

At minimum, one of:
- A warning log: `[telegram] plugin disabled: not in plugins.allow`
- `openclaw status` channels list showing `telegram: disabled (not in allowlist)` instead of empty
- `openclaw doctor` detecting configured channels that are not in `plugins.allow` and flagging it

**Ideal fix:** Bundled channel plugins with valid channel config (i.e., `channels.telegram.enabled: true`) should either bypass the allowlist check or be auto-added to `plugins.allow` when a channel

items.title: bug(plugin): Topic 会话中 session_status 报 Unknown sessionId，无法切换模型（#170 回归）
items.content:
### 现象

在 main agent 新建的 topic 会话中，用户要求切换模型时，Agent 调用 `session_status` 工具报错 **Unknown sessionId**，无法完成模型切换。

Agent 回复原文：「工具一直报 Unknown sessionId，这是个 bug——session_status 找不到当前 session。」

### 环境

- 客户端：Android coclaw app
- 实例：main易on腾讯云
- Topic：「虾天涯平台建设」
- 时间：2026-03-29 10:01
- 版本：v0.10.1（插件 v0.9.2）

### 复现步骤

1. 在 main agent 中新建一个 topic
2. 在 topic 中要求 Agent「切换当前 session 的模型为 claude-opus-4-6-thinking」
3. Agent 尝试调用 session_status 切换模型
4. 报错 Unknown sessionId

### 预期行为

Topic 会话的 sessionId 应正确注册到 OpenClaw sessions，session_status 工具应能识别并执行模型切换。

### 关联

- #170（Topic 会话未注册到 OpenClaw sessions）— 已关闭，本次为回归或修复不完整

items.author: Antsoldier1974

items.title: enhancement(ui): 模型 fallback 过程可视化——展示切换原因和过程，避免用户困惑
items.content:
### 现象

当 Agent 的主模型执行失败（超时/异常），OpenClaw 自动 fallback 到备用模型重新执行。当前 UI 直接将两次 run 的结果都显示为普通消息气泡，用户看到：

1. 第一条回复（失败模型）：显示「任务未完成」或异常内容
2. 第二条回复（fallback 模型）：正常回复

用户无法理解为什么同一条消息产生了两个回复，也不知道发生了模型切换。

### 复现场景

- 心跳 prompt 发送给 Agent
- 主模型 claude-sonnet-4-6 执行失败，回复「任务未完成」
- 自动 fallback 到 doubao-seed-2.0-code，回复 HEARTBEAT_OK
- UI 显示两条独立的回复气泡，无任何关联提示

### 期望行为

fallback 发生时，UI 应展示切换过程，例如：

- 在两次回复之间插入系统提示：🔄 claude-sonnet-4-6 执行失败，已自动切换至 doubao-seed-2.0-code
- 或将失败的 run 折叠显示（默认收起，点击可展开查看详情）
- 失败 run 的气泡可加视觉区分（灰色/半透明/删除线）

### 关联

- #194（Agent 超时后无重试详情）
- #152（工具调用缺乏过程反馈）

— [CoClaw Team](https://github.com/coclaw)
🤖 AI Agent? Visit [coclaw.net](https://www.coclaw.net) to install the CoClaw plugin and bind your owner.
items.author: Antsoldier1974

items.title: TTS: enforce standalone directive lines parsingJimmy/2026 03 29 01 48 34
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: TTS directives were previously parsed with regex-heavy matching, which made standalone-line requirements implicit and easy to misinterpret (especially for text blocks and mixed line endings).
- Why it matters: Ambiguous parsing can cause unexpected directive activation, inconsistent cleaned text behavior, and regression risk when input formatting varies (e.g., CRLF, extra blank lines, multiple text blocks).
- What changed:
  - Introduced internal line-by-line parsing helpers in `src/tts/directives.ts` with explicit states (`normal` / `in_tts_text_block`).
  - Made standalone directive rules explicit in behavior:
    - `[[tts:...]]` is recognized only when it occupies a full trimmed line.
    - `[[tts:text]]` and `[[/tts:text]]` are recognized only when each appears on its own line.
  - Preserved external API shape (`TtsDirectiveParseResult`) and compatibility of returned fields.
  - Added regression coverage in `src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts` for:
    - CRLF (`\r\n`) directive parsing.

## Summary

- Problem: OpenAI Codex OAuth login succeeds but model calls fail with "Missing scopes: model.request" (401).
- Why it matters: Users cannot use `openai-codex/gpt-5.4` (and similar) after OAuth.
- What changed: Updated the hardcoded OAuth scope in the `@mariozechner/pi-ai` dependency to include `model.request`.
- What did NOT change: No changes to OpenClaw provider plugins, auth flows, refresh logic, tests, or catalog.

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56658

## Change Type

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #56658

## Root Cause / Regression History

The `SCOPE` constant in `pi-ai`'s OpenAI Codex OAuth flow only requested `openid profile email offline_access`. The `model.request` scope is required by OpenAI for model inference on these tokens.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A (existing OAuth + `models status --probe` flow covers it; the scope is now included in the authorize URL).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

OAuth login for OpenAI Codex now requests the correct scope. Existing profiles will need to be re-authenticated (`openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex`).

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes (additional OAuth scope for model access)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No (same flow, just different scope param)
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: The scope is the documented requirement from OpenAI for these models. No broader permissions granted.

**Environment**
- OS: any
- Model/provider: openai-codex/gpt-5.4 (OAuth profile)

**Steps**
1. `openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex`
2. `openclaw models status --probe --probe-provider openai-codex`

**Expected**
No 401. Model calls succeed.

**Evidence**
- Before: authorize URL and callback lacked `model.request`
- After: scope includes it (verified in dependency)

- Updated scope in de

items.author: dewgie-bot

items.title: Feishu plugin tools re-register on every agent dispatch, producing excessive log noise
items.content:
## Description

The Feishu extension plugin tools (`feishu_doc`, `feishu_chat`, `feishu_wiki`, `feishu_drive`, `feishu_bitable`) re-register on **every agent dispatch**, not just on gateway startup. In a multi-agent setup, this produces significant log noise — approximately 4-5 registration log lines per agent per message.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw with the Feishu channel plugin and multiple agents (e.g. 5+ agents).
2. Send a message to any agent.
3. Observe gateway logs — all 5 Feishu tool registration messages appear for each agent that processes the dispatch.

## Log Evidence

```
[plugins] feishu_doc: Registered feishu_doc, feishu_app_scopes
[plugins] feishu_chat: Registered feishu_chat tool
[plugins] feishu_wiki: Registered feishu_wiki tool
[plugins] feishu_drive: Registered feishu_drive tool
[plugins] feishu_bitable: Registered bitable tools
[plugins] feishu_doc: Registered feishu_doc, feishu_app_scopes  <-- same tools, seconds later
[plugins] feishu_chat: Registered feishu_chat tool
...
```

In testing, `Registered feishu_doc` appeared **421 times** in a single day's log across ~99 unique minute-windows, averaging ~4.3 registrations per window. Each window corresponds to a message dispatch.

## Expected Behavior

Plugin tools should register **once** at gateway startup (or once per plugin lifecycle), not on every dispatch. Subsequent dispatches should reuse the already-registered tools without re-emitting registration logs.

## Actual Behavior

Every dispatch triggers full re-registration of all Feishu plugin tools, producing 5 INFO log lines per agent per message. With multiple agents configured, a single inbound message can produce 20-35 registration log lines.

## Impact

- **Log noise**: Makes it harder to find relevant log entries when debugging.
- **Performance**: Unnecessary re-initialization overhead on every message (though likely minor).
- The actual tool functionality is not affected.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Register tools

items.title: feat: add OpenClaw E2E verification platform
items.content:
## Summary

- Build a config-driven E2E testing platform for validating real OpenClaw agent user paths across `test`/`preview`/`prod` environments
- 5-layer architecture: Environment (YAML config), Runtime (Docker multi-instance), Prompt Source (imports real frontend `buildConnectBotPrompt()`), Scenario (YAML step definitions), Assertion (16 cross-channel checks across agent output, filesystem, DB, Hub API)
- First scenario: `quickstart_install_and_register` — parallel 2-instance install, `/botcord_env` switch, register, healthcheck, restart persistence

## Key decisions

- **Prompt is the real product source**: dynamically imports `frontend/src/lib/onboarding.ts:buildConnectBotPrompt()` so prompt regressions break E2E
- **Runtime matches deploy config**: `openclaw.json` uses the same `agents.defaults.model.primary` + `commands` + `session` + `gateway` structure as `~/openclaw_deploy/deploy-npm.sh`
- **Docker image pinned**: `alpine/openclaw:2026.3.7` (not `:latest`)
- **Environment switching**: `/botcord_env {preset}` step after install, before registration
- **Prod safety**: `allow_mutation: false` blocks write-heavy scenarios on prod

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
- [x] Verified `buildConnectBotPrompt()` import works at runtime with correct beta/stable URL resolution
- [x] CLI entry point loads and parses args correctly

items.author: zhangzhejian

items.title: Proactive OAuth refresh writes token to disk but does not restart Gateway
items.content:
## Bug Description

Proactive OAuth token refresh (introduced in [`1b8f8f2`](https://github.com/Neurons-AI/crawbot/commit/1b8f8f28ed631f3d49d655f35dba7210b791cbb0)) successfully refreshes the Anthropic OAuth access token and writes it to `auth-profiles.json`, but **does not restart the Gateway process** to pick up the new token.

This means the Gateway continues using the **old access token from RAM**, which eventually expires and causes all Anthropic API calls to fail.

## Root Cause

Commit [`06e62b6`](https://github.com/Neurons-AI/crawbot/commit/06e62b6cec1d42a572b36d16bb69681cd312c0ad) correctly added `gateway:restart` after **manual re-authentication** (UI login flow), but the **proactive refresh path** (`doRefresh` / function `w()` in minified code) was not updated to include the same restart.

The proactive refresh function currently:
1. ✅ Calls `refreshAccessToken()` via Anthropic token endpoint
2. ✅ Writes new token to `auth-profiles.json` via `writeAuthProfile()`
3. ✅ Schedules the next refresh via `scheduleNextRefresh()`
4. ❌ **Does NOT restart Gateway** — so Gateway never picks up the new token

## Evidence from Logs (CrawBot v2026.3.18)

```
# Proactive refresh succeeds, writes new token expiring at 02:25 UTC
[18:30:49] Proactive token refresh starting...
[18:30:49] Proactive refresh successful, new token written
[18:30:49] Scheduling proactive refresh in 465 min (expires: 2026-03-29T02:25:49.700Z)

# But Gateway still uses OLD token in RAM, fails hours before new token expires
[23:39:24] OAuth token refresh failed for anthropic
[00:09:24] All models failed: OAuth token refresh failed for anthropic
[00:30:30] All models failed: OAuth token refresh failed for anthropic
[00:43:31] All models failed: OAuth token refresh failed for anthropic

# User had to manually re-authenticate
[00:54:28] Starting Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow
[00:54:34] Token exchange successful
[00:54:34] Gateway restart requested   ← only manual re-auth triggers restart
```

Machine confi

items.title: feat(A14): Vertex AI parser spec — Vertex artifacts → IR
items.content:
## Summary

Adds `specs/vertex-parser-spec.md` — the specification for the Vertex AI → IR parser (reverse of the Vertex emitter). Implements the design contract for D23.

## What's covered

### Input formats
- `agent.json` (required — AgentShift-generated or Vertex AI API export)
- `tools.json` (optional — additional tool declarations from Vertex API)

### Field mapping
- Complete Vertex AI → IR field mapping table
- `displayName` → slugified `name`
- `goal` + `instructions` → `persona.system_prompt` reconstruction
- Section recovery from linearized `"SectionName:\ncontent"` patterns
- Function tools / OpenAPI tools / data store tools → `ir.tools` / `ir.knowledge`
- Auth reconstruction (apiKey, oauth2, service account → ToolAuth types)

### Governance extraction
- L1 guardrail heuristic on `instructions` array (shared pattern with Bedrock parser)
- `"Restrictions:"` instruction prefix → `sections["guardrails"]`

### Round-trip fidelity
Documents what survives (name, system_prompt, sections, language, tools) and what is lossy (triggers, constraints, tool_permissions).

### Shared utilities
Documents `src/agentshift/parsers/utils.py` — shared with Bedrock parser: `slugify`, `extract_guardrails_from_text`, `infer_guardrail_category/severity`, tool kind detection.

### CLI contract
`agentshift convert ./vertex-output/ --from vertex --to openclaw` and variants.

## Closes
A14 (Week 7 backlog)

items.title: infra: GCP billing disabled on end2end-spawn-openrouter project — all GCP E2E tests failing
items.content:
## Problem

All 9 GCP E2E tests fail because billing is disabled on the `end2end-spawn-openrouter` GCP project.

**Error from `gcloud`:**
```
ERROR: (gcloud.compute.instances.create) Could not fetch resource:
 - This API method requires billing to be enabled. Please enable billing on project
   #end2end-spawn-openrouter by visiting
   https://console.developers.google.com/billing/enable?project=end2end-spawn-openrouter
   then retry.
```

## Impact

GCP E2E: 0/9 agents pass (claude, openclaw, zeroclaw, codex, opencode, kilocode, hermes, junie, cursor all fail at provisioning).

## Fix

Enable billing at: https://console.developers.google.com/billing/enable?project=end2end-spawn-openrouter

This is an infrastructure/account issue, not a code bug. No code changes needed.

-- qa/e2e-tester

items.title: feat(cli): add --output json flag to run command
items.content:
## Summary
Adds a `--output json` / `-o json` flag to the `jamjet run` CLI command, enabling machine-readable output for scripting and CI pipelines.

Fixes #7

## Changes
- Added `--output` option accepting `text` (default) or `json`
- JSON output includes: `execution_id`, `final_state`, `steps_executed`, `total_duration_us`, `events`
- Suppresses Rich console formatting when `--output json` for clean stdout
- Always follows execution to completion in JSON mode (ignores `--no-follow`)
- Validates input — rejects invalid values with clear error message

## Example
```bash
# Human-readable (default)
jamjet run workflow.yaml

# Machine-readable JSON
jamjet run --output json workflow.yaml | jq '.final_state.status'
```

## Output Schema
```json
{
  "execution_id": "exec_abc123",
  "final_state": { ... },
  "steps_executed": 5,
  "total_duration_us": 1234567,
  "events": [ ... ]
}
```

## Testing
- [x] CLI flag appears in `--help`
- [x] Invalid `--output` values rejected with clear error
- [x] Syntax validation passed
- [x] Code follows existing patterns (Typer, async)

**核心问题**：OpenClaw 目前 Skill 分散在 ClawHub，缺乏场景化索引，用户难以找到「这类问题该用哪个 Skill」。

## 目标

构建 **OpenClaw Skill 场景索引站**，按场景而非工具分类：

| 场景 | 推荐 Skill |
|------|-----------|
| 代码审查 | subagent-review |
| 定时任务 | cron-manager |
| 浏览器自动化 | agent-browser |
| 文件处理 | baoyu-compress-image |
| 内容发布 | baoyu-post-to-x / wechat / weibo |

## 子任务

- [ ] 爬取 ClawHub 所有 Skill 元数据（名称、描述、触发词）
- [ ] 设计场景分类体系（参考 Codex 用例库）
- [ ] 建立「问题 → Skill」映射表（关键词匹配 + AI 归类）
- [ ] 产出静态站点或 Notion 数据库
- [ ] 编写 Skill：根据用户问题推荐最合适的 Skill

## 预期收益

- 降低新用户入门门槛
- 提高 Skill 复用率
- 可作为 OpenClaw 官方文档的补充场景索引

items.title: [agento] fix(skeptic): mock ghJsonPaginate in mergeGate tests
items.content:
## Summary

Fix test failures in the skeptic mergeGate tests introduced in PR #267.

### Root Cause

The skeptic verdict parsing section of `mergeGate.ts` calls `ghJsonPaginate` (not `ghJson`) for the issues/comments endpoint, to handle large comment histories via pagination. The existing mocks only covered `ghJson` and `fetchReviews`, leaving `ghJsonPaginate` to invoke the real `gh api` command — which returns `{}` (unauthenticated), causing the verdict loop to throw `TypeError: undefined is not iterable` (silently caught), leaving `skepticVerdict` as `null`.

### Fix

Add `ghJsonPaginate` to the `vi.mock` factory with a mutable `mockState` object shared between the factory (run once) and per-test setup:

```typescript
const mockState = {
  ghJsonValues: [],
  ghJsonPaginateValues: [],
  fetchReviewsResult: [],
};
```

The mock functions use `mockImplementation` that reads from this shared state, allowing each test to configure the correct values for both `ghJson` (PR data, commit status, review threads) and `ghJsonPaginate` (check-runs, verdict comments).

## Test plan
- [x] All 12 mergeGate skeptic verdict tests pass locally
- [ ] CI Test check passes
- [ ] CodeRabbit review clears

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Modifies CI workflows (including a `pull_request_target` gate restriction and gitleaks install integrity checks) and changes merge-gate state derivation/parsing, which could affect PR gating behavior if incorrect. Most changes are additive with updated tests, but mistakes could cause false PASS/FAIL/SKIPPED outcomes in automation.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Hardens skeptic merge-gate evaluation and fixes its tests.** `fetchMergeGateState` now uses a new `ghJsonPaginate` helper to reliably page through check runs and issue comments, returns additional raw CI/mergeability fields, and parses verdicts via a shared `VERDICT_LINE_RE` that accepts common markdown-wrapped form

items.content:
## 背景

飞书开源 lark-cli，支持 AI Agent，覆盖 11 个业务域。这代表了一种趋势：**CLI 封装是让外部 AI 系统安全调用内部工具的最佳实践**。

OpenClaw 目前通过 Gateway API 和 Skill 系统暴露能力，但缺少一个**轻量 CLI 封装层**，让：
- 第三方 Agent（Claude Code、Codex 等）可以通过 CLI 调用 OpenClaw 工具
- 无需了解 OpenClaw 内部实现，只通过标准输入输出交互
- 所有调用经过 OpenClaw Hook 安全审计

## 目标

实现 `openclaw agent-cli` 子命令：
```bash
openclaw agent-cli call <tool-name> --args <json>
openclaw agent-cli list-tools
openclaw agent-cli stream <session-id>
```

## 子任务

- [ ] 设计 CLI 封装层的工具注册协议（基于现有 Tool registry）
- [ ] 实现 `openclaw agent-cli` 命令行接口
- [ ] 接入 OpenClaw Hook 系统：每次 CLI 调用都经过权限审计
- [ ] 支持流式输出（SSE 模式）
- [ ] 编写 Skill：将 Claude Code 接入 OpenClaw Agent CLI
- [ ] 文档：让外部 Agent 通过 CLI 调用 OpenClaw 的集成指南

## 预期收益

- 扩展 OpenClaw 作为 **AI Agent 中间层**的能力边界
- 支持多 Agent 协作：Claude Code + Codex + OpenClaw 工具链
- 代码量预估：500-800 行，难度中
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: Implement OpenAI Codex OAuth login for Python
items.content:
## Context

CommandClaw needs to authenticate with OpenAI's API using ChatGPT subscription credentials (Codex OAuth), not just API keys. This enables users with ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Team/Enterprise to use their subscription for agent runs.

## How Codex CLI handles auth

The Codex CLI (Rust, [openai/codex](https://github.com/openai/codex)) supports three auth modes:

1. **`chatgpt`** — Browser-based OAuth flow. Server returns an `authUrl`, client opens it in browser, user signs in, callback completes the flow. Stores tokens in `~/.codex/auth.json`.
2. **`chatgptDeviceCode`** — Device code flow. Server returns a `userCode` + `verificationUrl`, user enters the code after signing in. Better for headless/remote environments.
3. **`apiKey`** — Direct API key. Simple but requires separate billing.

Credential storage:
- **File**: `~/.codex/auth.json` — `{"tokens": {"access_token": "...", "refresh_token": "..."}}`
- **macOS Keychain**: `"Codex Auth"` entry keyed by SHA256 of `$CODEX_HOME` path

Tokens are treated as valid for 1 hour from file mtime, then refreshed.

## How OpenClaw handles it

OpenClaw (TypeScript) delegates to `@mariozechner/pi-ai`'s `loginOpenAICodex()` — a closed-source JS function. It:
- Opens browser to OpenAI auth page
- Listens on `localhost:1455` for the OAuth callback
- Returns `OAuthCredentials` (access_token, refresh_token, expires)
- Supports VPS mode (paste redirect URL manually)

## What CommandClaw needs

### Phase 1 (immediate) — Read existing credentials
Just read `~/.codex/auth.json` if the user has Codex CLI installed. Zero OAuth implementation needed.

```python
# ~/.codex/auth.json
{"tokens": {"access_token": "eyJ...", "refresh_token": "v1|..."}}
```

### Phase 2 (soon) — Native OAuth dance
Find or build a Python package that implements the Codex OAuth flow. Options:

1. **Find existing Python package** — search for anything that implements OpenAI ChatGPT OAuth / PKCE flow
2. **Port from Codex CLI** — the Rust code is at `codex-rs/` in the

items.author: dragonfire1119

items.title: Feature: Allow reaction events to optionally trigger agent turns
items.content:
## Summary

Emoji reactions on Slack (and likely other channels) are received by the gateway and queued as system events, but they only surface to the agent on the **next turn** (triggered by a new message or heartbeat). They don't trigger their own agent turn.

## Use Case

When a user reacts to an agent's message with an emoji (e.g. thumbs-up, heart, etc.), the agent should be able to see and respond to that reaction in near-real-time — not just on the next unrelated turn.

**Concrete workflow:** User reacts positively to work delivered by a sub-agent → orchestrator agent sees the reaction → proactively relays feedback to the sub-agent → reports back to the user. This "feedback loop" pattern is central to multi-agent teams but currently requires the user to type a message instead of just reacting.

## Current Behavior

- `reactionNotifications: "all"` is set (Slack channel config)
- `reaction_added` is subscribed in the Slack app bot events
- Reactions are mapped into system events (per docs)
- System events are queued and injected as `System:` lines on the **next agent turn**
- Reactions do **not** trigger their own agent turn

## Proposed Behavior

Add an optional configuration (e.g. `reactionTriggersTurn: true` or `reactionWakeMode: "now" | "next-turn" | "off"`) that allows reaction events to immediately trigger an agent turn, similar to how inbound messages do.

Default could remain `next-turn` (current behavior) for backward compatibility.

## Environment

- OpenClaw v2026.3.24
- Slack channel (Socket Mode)
- `channels.slack.reactionNotifications: "all"`

## Impact

This would make emoji reactions a first-class input for agent interaction, enabling tighter feedback loops in multi-agent workflows without requiring users to type messages for simple approval/acknowledgment signals.
items.author: potterdigital

items.title: Feature/ssh tunnel gateway
items.content:
## Summary

This PR delivers two primary outcomes:

1. SSH tunnel support in the tray app so gateway traffic can run through a local loopback endpoint while forwarding to a remote host.
2. Reliable Quick Chat (Quick Send) by treating the tray app as a paired device identity and aligning with gateway auth/approval behavior.

## Key Additions

### 1) SSH Tunnel Feature

- Added persistent SSH tunnel settings (enable flag, SSH user/host, local/remote ports).
- Added effective gateway URL behavior so tunnel mode uses local loopback (`ws://127.0.0.1:<localPort>`) while preserving remote target configuration.
- Added tunnel lifecycle management in the tray app (start/stop, restart behavior, and shutdown cleanup).
- Added settings UI and validation/testing flow for tunnel configuration.

### 2) Quick Chat / Quick Send Working with Paired Tray Device

- Added operator device identity handshake payload (signed device info on connect).
- Added connect signature compatibility modes and fallback handling.
- Persisted/rehydrated device token from successful handshake for reconnect continuity.
- Added explicit pairing-required handling path (including reconnect suppression behavior where appropriate).
- Hardened `chat.send` flow with tracked request/response handling, session key usage, and idempotency key.
- Updated Quick Send error UX to provide targeted remediation for:
  - pairing required / not paired
  - missing operator scopes

## Other Fixes and Updates (Complete Branch Coverage)

### Connection and Reconnect Behavior

- Improved shared websocket reconnect behavior to avoid getting stuck after transient connect failures.
- Added reconnect-loop guarding and safer reconnect/dispose behavior in the websocket base client.
- Added `ShouldAutoReconnect()` control path for terminal/approval states.

### UI / UX Improvements

- Improved Quick Send dialog behavior and focus handling.
- Added richer Quick Send error details/copy guidance behavior.
- Fixed canvas visibility by forci
items.author: sytone

items.title: Prevent agent from modifying openclaw.json and gateway config
items.content:
## Problem

The OpenClaw agent runs as the `node` user (uid 1000) in SPCS and has full filesystem write access. It can modify `openclaw.json` at runtime — including channel allowlists, model settings, and security-sensitive config. Changes persist on the volume across restarts.

A compromised or misbehaving agent could:
- Remove `allowFrom` restrictions on messaging channels
- Add new provider endpoints or alter model config
- Modify credentials or pairing allowlists

## Solution: Root-Owned Config + Entrypoint Permission Enforcement

Use OS-level file permissions to make sensitive files read-only for the `node` user. The entrypoint runs as `root` to set permissions, then drops to `node` for the gateway process.

### Architecture

- `openclaw.json` lives on a **Snowflake stage-backed volume** (not baked into the image)
- It is uploaded via `snowclaw push --config` and appears at `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` on the volume
- The entrypoint runs as `root`, locks down the file, then drops to `node`
- Config changes require a `snowclaw push --config` + service restart (option c — no runtime config editing)

### What Gets Protected (root:node, 440)

- `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` — gateway config
- `~/.openclaw/credentials/` — channel creds, pairing allowlists
- `~/.openclaw/secrets.json` — if present

### What Stays Writable (node:node)

- `~/.openclaw/workspace/` — agent workspace (memory, code, notes)
- `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/` — session transcripts

### Defense in Depth

Also set `tools.fs.workspaceOnly: true` in openclaw.json so native file tools are restricted to the workspace directory. Two independent layers:
1. **OS permissions** — blocks writes to protected files regardless of method
2. **Tool policy** — restricts native file tools to workspace only

### Config Change Workflow

Config changes go through **SnowClaw CLI** (`snowclaw push --config`) **→ service restart**. No runtime config editing from the gateway UI (acceptable tradeoff for security in SPCS)
items.author: JacobScott98

items.title: [codex] add agent-friendly cli help and json contract
items.content:
## Summary

I tightened `ghcrawl`'s CLI contract for agent use and discoverability.

- I replaced the single top-level usage block with per-command help, including `ghcrawl help <command>`, `<command> --help`, and `<command> -h`
- I added an explicit usage/runtime exit-code contract and command-specific help hints for validation failures
- I added explicit `--json` support to the public agent-facing commands while keeping current JSON-by-default behavior as a compatibility path
- I added global `--config-path` and `--workspace-root` overrides and threaded them through config loading and `init`
- I updated the README, spec, and skill/protocol docs to describe the explicit machine contract

## Why

I was reviewing guidance for CLIs that AI agents can use reliably, and `ghcrawl` was missing several of the highest-value pieces:

- subcommand-local help
- a clearer machine contract for errors and exit codes
- a consistent explicit JSON mode across agent-facing commands
- deterministic CLI-level config selection overrides

This change brings the project closer to that guidance without breaking current callers in the same pass.

## Impact

- Humans and agents can now discover command syntax locally without guessing flags or reading source
- Agent wrappers can distinguish usage errors from runtime failures more reliably
- The documented JSON contract is now explicit instead of only implied by current defaults
- Config and workspace resolution can be forced from the CLI for automation and testing

## Validation

I verified this with:

- `pnpm --filter ghcrawl test`
- `pnpm --filter ghcrawl typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ghcrawl/api-contract build`
- `pnpm --filter @ghcrawl/api-core build`

items.author: hnshah

items.title: [Repo Assist] eng: add global.json to pin .NET SDK to 10.0.x (rollForward: latestFeature)
items.content:
🤖 *This is an automated draft PR from Repo Assist.*

## Summary

Adds a `global.json` at the repository root to pin the .NET SDK to `10.0.x`.

## Problem

Without a `global.json`, any .NET SDK version installed on the machine is used to build the project. This creates two risks:

1. **Version drift between machines** — a developer on SDK 10.0.200 and CI on 10.0.102 may see subtly different compiler diagnostics, code generation, or NuGet resolution behaviour.
2. **Accidental forward jumps** — once .NET 11 SDKs are installed alongside .NET 10, builds silently switch to .NET 11 without anyone noticing, until something breaks.

## Fix

```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "10.0.102",
    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
  }
}
```

`rollForward: latestFeature` means:
- ✅ Patch updates (`10.0.103`, `10.0.200`) are accepted automatically — no manual `global.json` bump for routine SDK updates.
- ✅ Feature bands within .NET 10 (`10.0.200`, `10.0.300`) are accepted.
- ❌ A jump to .NET 11 requires an explicit version update in `global.json` — intentional breakage prevention.

This matches the approach recommended by the .NET team for reproducible builds and is standard practice in mature .NET repositories.

> **Note**: This work was originally blocked in issue #106 due to protected-file restrictions. The branch and `global.json` content were already validated in that run.

## Test Status

✅ 503/18 shared tests pass/skipped  
✅ 93/0 Tray tests pass

Closes #106

items.title: [Repo Assist] fix: reset node/gateway state on settings save; clear _isPendingApproval on disconnect
items.content:
🤖 *This is an automated draft PR from Repo Assist.*

## Summary

Two related bugs caused the tray to misreport connection state in node mode, contributing to the symptoms described in #114 ("Status: Connected, Node Mode: Disconnected").

## Root causes

### 1. Stale `_currentStatus` and non-null `_gatewayClient` after settings save

`OnSettingsSaved` disposed the old gateway client but did **not**:
- call `UnsubscribeGatewayEvents()` before disposing
- null out `_gatewayClient`
- reset `_currentStatus`

Result: if the user was previously in operator mode (Status: Connected), switching to node mode via Settings left the tray showing **"Status: Connected"** while the node connection was still establishing — showing **"Node Mode: Disconnected"**. The two contradictory indicators come directly from this stale state.

Additionally, `_gatewayClient != null` remaining true caused code paths like `ShowTrayMenuPopup`'s health-check fetch and `ShowStatusDetail` to proceed with a disposed client.

### 2. `_isPendingApproval` not reset on connection drop

`WindowsNodeClient.OnDisconnected` and `OnError` reset `_isConnected` but not `_isPendingApproval`. After a connection drop + reconnect, the tray would show **"Waiting for Approval"** until hello-ok was received again, even though the pairing state is unknown during the reconnect gap. Now both flags are reset together.

## Changes

**`App.xaml.cs` — `OnSettingsSaved`**
- Call `UnsubscribeGatewayEvents()` before `Dispose()`
- Set `_gatewayClient = null` after disposal
- Reset `_currentStatus = ConnectionStatus.Disconnected` and call `UpdateTrayIcon()` so the tray reflects the transition immediately

**`WindowsNodeClient.cs` — `OnDisconnected` / `OnError`**
- Also reset `_isPendingApproval = false` alongside `_isConnected = false`

## Test Status

✅ 503 shared tests pass, 18 skipped  
✅ 93 Tray tests pass

Closes #114

items.title: fix: Stripe webhook crash + Telegram manual fallback
items.content:
## Stripe webhook (the actual bug)
The `handleCheckoutCompleted` handler crashed with Postgres error `42P10: no unique constraint matching ON CONFLICT`. The `subscriptions` table was missing a unique constraint on `user_id`, so `upsert({ onConflict: 'user_id' })` always failed. This crashed the entire webhook handler, meaning the user's plan was never updated after payment.

**Fix:** Replaced upsert with explicit select-then-insert/update. Also added a migration SQL for the unique constraint.

## Telegram
BotFather automation was checking raw `process.env` values that could have whitespace, causing it to fall through to the manual setup path. Switched to trimmed values.

items.title: feat(imessage): native macOS iMessage adapter
items.content:
## Summary

Adds iMessage as a messaging platform for instar agents running on macOS. Messages are received by polling the native `~/Library/Messages/chat.db` SQLite database and sent via the `imsg` CLI tool from Claude Code sessions.

- **NativeBackend**: Read-only macOS Messages database integration using better-sqlite3. Polls for new messages by tracking max ROWID, formats conversation context for session bootstrap. Uses `query_only` pragma (not `readonly`) to read the WAL where Messages.app writes new data.
- **IMessageAdapter**: Primary messaging adapter implementing the MessagingAdapter interface. Handles authorization (allowlist), message deduplication, logging (JSONL), session mapping, and stall detection.
- **IMessageRpcClient**: JSON-RPC 2.0 client for the `imsg` CLI tool (send-only path, since LaunchAgents lack AppleScript Automation permission).
- **Session routing** (`wireIMessageRouting`): Routes incoming iMessages to Claude Code sessions following the same inject-or-spawn pattern as Telegram. Includes `buildIMessageBootstrap()` which gives sessions explicit relay instructions and session persistence guidance.
- **Session spawn fixes**: `waitForClaudeReady()` was timing out at 30s with narrow prompt detection. Now uses 90s timeout, detects 4 readiness signals (`❯`, "bypass permissions", `/effort`, model indicator), captures a wider window (20 lines vs 5), and has a two-phase retry with 15s grace period.
- **HTTP endpoints**: `/imessage/status`, `/imessage/reply/:recipient`, `/imessage/chats`, `/imessage/chats/:chatId/history`, `/imessage/search`, `/imessage/log-stats`
- **Reply script**: `imessage-reply.sh` sends via `imsg send` and notifies the server for logging + stall tracking

### Prerequisites for users

- macOS with iMessage configured (Apple ID / iCloud identity)
- Full Disk Access granted to the terminal or LaunchAgent running instar (for reading `chat.db`)
- [`imsg`](https://github.com/xharris/imsg) CLI installed (`cargo install imsg`) for se

items.title: [agento] fix(skeptic): match markdown-bold VERDICT format in mergeGate.ts
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes multiple skeptic tooling issues found during integration testing:

- **gh-client.ts**: Add `ghJsonPaginate()` that handles NDJSON output from `gh api --paginate` (single JSON doc fast path + multi-line flat-array fallback). Fixes pagination bugs where `gh api --paginate` emits one JSON object per line.
- **mergeGate.ts**: Fix VERDICT regex to accept plain `VERDICT: PASS` (make bold markdown markers optional with `(?:\*{1,2})?`). Previously required `**VERDICT:**` format.
- **skeptic.ts**: Import+reexport `VERDICT_LINE_RE` from `verdict-utils.js` — single source of truth, removes duplicate definition.
- **skeptic-gate.yml**: Add `GITHUB_TOKEN` to `post_trigger` step env; update `VERDICT_JQ` to use `\s*:` for optional whitespace before colon.
- **security.yml**: Add explicit ref conditional (`pr sha || github.sha`) for non-PR events.
- **prompt.ts**: Truncate checkRuns to top 20 entries to avoid prompt bloat.

## Testing

- Manual: `ao skeptic verify -n 267 --dry-run` on PR #267
- CI: Skeptic Gate passes with `VERDICT: PASS`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

> **Medium Risk**
> Touches merge-gating logic and GitHub Actions workflows that control CI/PR gating; parsing or API-pagination mistakes could cause incorrect PASS/FAIL decisions or flaky gates. Other changes are documentation-only but the workflow/merge-gate updates warrant extra review.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Skeptic merge-gate behavior is tightened and made more robust.** Verdict parsing now accepts markdown-wrapped/prefixed formats (e.g. `## VERDICT: PASS`, `**VERDICT: FAIL**`) while `llm-eval` remains *fail-closed* by only treating PASS/FAIL as valid (SKIPPED is explicitly invalid for merge decisions). Merge-gate state gathering is expanded to include raw commit status, per-check-run summaries, and full pagination for review threads/check runs; the prompt output is updated to surface these raw signals.

items.title: [Bug]: WebSocket webchat connections drop with code 1006 during long tool calls — no server-side ping/keepalive
items.content:
## Bug Description

The dashboard webchat WebSocket connection silently drops (close code 1006) during long tool call chains. Messages are buffered server-side and flood in when the user sends a new message (which opens a fresh connection).

## Root Cause Analysis

Gateway logs show **zero WebSocket ping/pong keepalive frames**. During tool calls, no data flows on the WebSocket for 30-60s, causing network intermediaries to treat the connection as idle and kill it.

Evidence from gateway logs:

```
[ws] webchat disconnected code=1006 reason=n/a conn=<id1>
[ws] webchat disconnected code=1006 reason=n/a conn=<id2>
[ws] webchat disconnected code=1006 reason=n/a conn=<id3>
[ws] webchat disconnected code=1006 reason=n/a conn=<id4>
```

12 disconnects in 17 minutes, each with a unique connection ID — the client reconnects but keeps getting killed.

Grepping logs for `ping`, `keepalive`, or `heartbeat` returns zero WebSocket-related results, confirming the server does not send WS ping frames.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.3.24
- Connection: Tailscale direct WireGuard tunnel (not DERP relay, not Funnel)
- Both machines on same LAN, direct peering confirmed
- OS: Linux server, Windows client
- Browser: Chrome

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open dashboard webchat
2. Send a message that triggers multiple tool calls (e.g., browser automation, exec chains)
3. Wait 30-60s while tools execute
4. Observe: chat stops updating, no new messages appear
5. Send another message
6. All buffered messages flood in at once

## Expected Behavior

WebSocket connection should stay alive during tool execution. Server should send periodic ping frames (every 15-30s) to prevent intermediaries from killing idle connections.

## Actual Behavior

WebSocket dies silently (code 1006) during idle periods. Messages are persisted but not delivered until a new connection is established.

## Suggested Fix

Add server-side WebSocket ping frames at a 15-30s interval on webchat connections. This is standard WebSo
items.author: joelagnel

items.title: feat(config): deploy exec-approvals.json seed on first install
items.content:
## Problem

When switching `tools.exec.security` from `full` → `allowlist` (see tardigrde/openclaw-prod#45), the allowlist starts empty. Every command the agent tries to run triggers a Telegram approval prompt — common tools like `git`, `npm`, `docker`, `python3`, etc. all need manual approval before the first use. This creates significant UX friction over the first few sessions.

## Solution

Two mechanisms together eliminate cold-start friction without compromising security:

### 1. `autoAllowSkills: true`
When enabled in `exec-approvals.json`, executables referenced by known skills are automatically allowlisted. This covers the long tail of skill-specific CLIs without manual intervention.

### 2. Pre-seeded allowlist (`config/exec-approvals.json`)
A seed file with common binaries from the standard Docker image pre-approved:
- `/usr/local/bin/*` — node, npm, npx, openclaw, sops, uv, uvx, clawhub, etc.
- `/usr/bin/git`, `/usr/bin/python3`, `/usr/bin/gh`, `/usr/bin/curl`, `/usr/bin/ssh`
- `/bin/bash`, `/bin/sh`

Ansible deploys this **only when `exec-approvals.json` does not already exist** on the VPS, so existing installs with a built-up allowlist are never overwritten.

## How allowlist + approvals work

```
tools.exec.security = allowlist   ← set in openclaw.json
tools.exec.ask      = on-miss     ← set in openclaw.json

exec-approvals.json (on the execution host, mounted into the container):
  defaults.autoAllowSkills = true  ← auto-approves skill CLIs
  agents.main.allowlist    = [...]  ← pre-approved common binaries
```

The allowlist lives in `~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json` on the host (mapped into the container via the `$OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR` volume). It cannot be configured inside `openclaw.json` — only the security mode and ask behavior live there.

When a command misses the allowlist, the gateway sends a Telegram DM:
```
Exec approval requested:
  Command: docker compose up -d
  Agent: main

[Allow once] [Always allow] [Deny]
```

Approving with **Always
items.author: tardigrde

items.title: [Bug]: WhatsApp cross-chat outbound rejects valid E.164 targets in OpenClaw 2026.3.24
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

In OpenClaw 2026.3.24, WhatsApp cross-chat outbound delivery fails by rejecting a valid E.164 target with GatewayClientRequestError: Error: Delivering to WhatsApp requires target <E.164|group JID>, even though current-chat replies still work.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Link WhatsApp successfully in OpenClaw so replies in the current chat work.
2. Run:
bash
openclaw message send --channel whatsapp --target +5493517894500 --message "Prueba cross-chat"

3. Observe the command fail with:
text
GatewayClientRequestError: Error: Delivering to WhatsApp requires target <E.164|group JID>


### Expected behavior

A WhatsApp message to another contact should be accepted and sent when using a valid E.164 target, consistent with the CLI/help text that states WhatsApp recipients use E.164 targets, e.g.:

bash
openclaw message send --target +15555550123 --message "Hi"


In this case, the concrete expected result is that:

bash
openclaw message send --channel whatsapp --target +5493517894500 --message "Prueba cross-chat"


should send the message successfully instead of rejecting the target.

### Actual behavior

Sending a WhatsApp message to another contact outside the current chat fails instead of delivering.

Observed CLI result:
text
GatewayClientRequestError: Error: Delivering to WhatsApp requires target <E.164|group JID>


This was reproduced with:
bash
openclaw message send --channel whatsapp --target +5493517894500 --message "Prueba cross-chat"


The same cross-chat failure was also observed from the agent message tool, while replies in the current active WhatsApp chat continued to work.

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw 2026.3.24 (cff6dc9)

### Operating system

Linux 6.1.0-41-amd64 (x64) with Node 22.22.0

### Install method

nstalled globally via npm / package manager, with the runtime located under /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw.

### Model

gpt-5.4

### Provider

items.title: [agento] fix(design-doc): strip full CURSOR_SUMMARY block — prevent orphan closing tags
items.content:
## Summary

- Fix `extractDescription()` in `scripts/generate-pr-design-docs.mjs` to strip the **full** `CURSOR_SUMMARY` block (opening marker + content + closing marker) in one pass
- This prevents the closing tag from being orphaned as visible text in generated `.html` design docs
- Keep the fallback HTML comment stripper non-greedy per-comment using `[\s\S]*?` with global replacement

## Root cause

The previous patterns stripped individual comment markers (`<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->`) and their entity-encoded variants.  For PR bodies containing a **paired** CURSOR_SUMMARY block:

```
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---
...content...
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
```

the greedy `<!--[\s\S]*?-->` fallback stops at the `>` of `<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->`, removing only the opening marker and leaving the closing `<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->` as orphaned text rendered in the HTML output.  This is what CodeRabbit reports as `CURSOR_SUMMARY` leaking into the design doc.

## Changes

- `scripts/generate-pr-design-docs.mjs`: Replace individual comment-strip patterns with full-block patterns matching from `<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY` (or `<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY`) through the matching `-->`, covering all four known encodings
- `docs/design/pr-designs/pr-202.{md,html}`: Regenerated with fix applied — confirmed `CURSOR_SUMMARY` absent

## Testing

- `node scripts/generate-pr-design-docs.mjs --single-pr=202 --force` → CURSOR_SUMMARY 0 occurrences in `pr-202.html`
- gitleaks scan: clean

## Evidence

**Claim class**: unit (regex fix — no runtime code change)

**Verdict**: PASS

**Terminal output**: CURSOR_SUMMARY block absent from generated design doc:
```bash
$ grep -c CURSOR_SUMMARY docs/design/pr-designs/pr-202.html
0   # CURSOR_SUMMARY absent after fix
```

Closes orch-b19

items.title: Fix SandboxCheckpointSchema missing fields causing config validation errors
items.content:
`SandboxCheckpointSchema` used `.strict()` but omitted four fields that exist in `CheckpointConfig`/`SandboxCheckpointSettings` and are used at runtime, causing immediate config rejection when users set them in `openclaw.json`.

## Summary

- **Problem:** `SandboxCheckpointSchema` in `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` was missing `checkpointStride`, `adaptiveStride`, `maxTotalSizeBytes`, and `memoryCheckpoint` — the `.strict()` mode turned any config using these into a hard validation error.
- **Why it matters:** Users setting valid, documented checkpoint config options got an `Unrecognized keys` error and checkpoint config silently did nothing even when no error was shown.
- **What changed:** Added the four fields to the Zod schema; updated `resolveCheckpointConfig` in `src/agents/sandbox/config.ts` to pass them through to the runtime (they were being silently dropped).
- **What did NOT change:** `SandboxCheckpointSettings` in `src/config/types.sandbox.ts` already had all four fields — no type changes needed.

```typescript
// Before — four fields silently rejected or dropped
export const SandboxCheckpointSchema = z.object({
  enabled, strategy, onlyMutating, maxSnapshots, ttlMs, skipTools,
}).strict().optional();

// After — all runtime fields accepted and forwarded
export const SandboxCheckpointSchema = z.object({
  enabled, strategy, onlyMutating, maxSnapshots, ttlMs, skipTools,
  checkpointStride: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
  adaptiveStride: z.boolean().optional(),
  maxTotalSizeBytes: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
  memoryCheckpoint: z.boolean().optional(),
}).strict().optional();
```

- [ ] Chore/infra

items.author: Copilot

items.title: [Bug]: exec tool not exposed to agent when using openai-completions providers (Mistral, Ollama)`
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

## Bug Description

When using providers that go through the `openai-completions` adapter (Mistral, Ollama), the `exec` tool is not available to the agent. The agent cannot run shell commands, which means CLI-based skills like `gog` (Gmail/Calendar) are non-functional despite being marked as "ready" in `openclaw skills`.

The agent consistently responds with "I don't have access to your email system" or "My tools are limited to workspace file operations" — it never attempts to call `exec` because the tool isn't in its tool list.

### Steps to reproduce

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Fresh OpenClaw install (Docker: `openclaw-custom:latest` built from `main` @ `ab2ef7bbfc`)
2. Configure Mistral as provider (`openai-completions` adapter) with `mistral-small-latest`
3. Set `tools.profile: "coding"` (includes `group:runtime` which should provide `exec`)
4. Set `tools.exec.host: "gateway"` and `tools.exec.security: "allowlist"`
5. Install `gog` CLI in container, verify `openclaw skills` shows gog as "ready"
6. Send any message requesting email access via Slack DM (e.g., "check my last email")
7. Agent responds that it has no email access — never attempts `exec`

Also tested with:
- Ollama provider (`qwen3.5:9b-q4_K_M`) — same behavior
- `tools.profile: "full"` — same behavior
- System prompt explicitly telling the agent about `gog` and `exec` — same behavior
- TOOLS.md with gog command examples in workspace — same behavior



### Expected behavior

## Expected Behavior

The `exec` tool should be available to the agent when `tools.profile` includes `group:runtime` (which contains `exec`), regardless of whether the provider uses `openai-completions`, `openai-responses`, or `anthropic` API adapters. The Chat Completions API format used by `openai-completions` supports function calling / tools.



### Actual behavior

The agent never receives the exec tool in its tool lis

items.title: fix(whatsapp): support quoted outbound replies
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp outbound sends already carry `replyToId` through the generic delivery pipeline, but the WhatsApp adapters dropped it before the Baileys send call, so replies were always sent as new messages.
- Why it matters: group follow-ups and confirmations lose the native quoted-reply bubble, which makes automated replies much harder to follow in busy chats.
- What changed: forwarded `replyToId` through both WhatsApp outbound adapter paths, added a bounded recent-message cache for inbound/outbound `WAMessage`s, and used Baileys `quoted` sends when the target message is still available; added regression tests around forwarding and quoted-send behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new config knobs, no changes to inbound reply parsing, and no attempt to reconstruct quoted replies for messages outside the recent cache window.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

- [ ] Auth / tokens

- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #56465
- Related #56465
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: `replyToId` reached the WhatsApp outbound surface, but neither `src/channels/plugins/whatsapp-shared.ts` nor `extensions/whatsapp/src/outbound-adapter.ts` forwarded it into `sendMessageWhatsApp`, and the WhatsApp send API never resolved a Baileys `quoted` message.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression test that asserted WhatsApp preserved `replyToId` all the way into the final socket send options.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): the generic outbound plumbing and inbound reply-context extraction already supported `replyToId`; the gap was specif

## Summary

Steps 7-9 now respect `execution_mode: direct` — skip HubSpot uploads when running locally without tokens.

## Root Cause

Steps 7/8 passed `--upload` to image generator scripts which require `HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN` from `openclaw.json`. In direct mode (local dev), no tokens available → scripts crash → Step 9 blocks on missing `image-manifest.json`.

## Changes

|------|--------|-------|
| 7 (OG Image) | Always `--upload` | `--upload` only in agent mode |
| 8 (Image Optimization) | Always `--upload` | `--upload` only in agent mode |
| 9 (HubSpot Upload) | Always POST to MC API | Skip POST in direct mode (QA gates still run) |
| `_ensure_image_manifest_ready` | Hard block if missing | Return `[]` in direct mode |

OpenClaw 今日呈现**高活跃度、高压力**态势：24小时内 Issues 和 PR 各更新 500 条，社区讨论密度极高。核心事件是 **v2026.3.28-beta.1 版本发布**，包含重大破坏性变更（Qwen OAuth 废弃）。与此同时，主分支因 `pi-coding-agent 0.63.0` 升级引发**严重回归**——嵌入式 Agent 认证失效，导致大量用户遭遇 "No API key for provider" 错误，维护团队已紧急合并多个修复 PR。Linux/Windows 原生客户端需求 (#75) 持续升温（58 评论），成为社区最期待的功能缺口。

items.title: [Bug] Egress proxy fails to relay WebSocket frames after successful HTTP CONNECT + 101 Switching Protocols
items.content:
### Agent Diagnostic

- Pointed coding agent at the OpenShell repository and inspected the relevant files: `crates/openshell-sandbox/src/l7/rest.rs`, `relay.rs`, `provider.rs`, and `mod.rs`
- Identified that `is_bodiless_response()` treats 101 Switching Protocols as a generic 1xx informational response. After forwarding the 101 headers, the relay loop returns to HTTP parsing instead of switching to raw TCP passthrough.
- Confirmed that `access: full` only expands to broad HTTP-level rules (`rule_json("*", "**")` in `expand_access_presets()`). It does not provide true raw TCP passthrough — L7 inspection continues inside the CONNECT tunnel.
- Verified with a raw Python socket test: the `HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols` response arrives correctly, but all subsequent WebSocket frames are dropped (timeout). The same test run directly from the host (bypassing the proxy) receives both the 101 and the WebSocket frames immediately.
- Proxy logs confirm continued L7 inspection even after the upgrade: `[openshell_sandbox::l7::relay] HTTP_REQUEST ...`
- Wrote and tested a patch: detect 101 in `relay_response()`, added `RelayOutcome::Upgraded` variant, and switch to `tokio::io::copy_bidirectional` after upgrade. All 322 existing tests pass + new test for 101 behavior.
- Built the patched binary (cross-compiled for linux/arm64 via Docker), deployed it to the K3s container via hostPath, and verified that WebSocket frames now relay end-to-end through the proxy.
- Consulted Grok (xAI) for architectural guidance on the multi-hop networking and security implications throughout the investigation.

### Description

**Summary**

When an application inside an OpenShell sandbox establishes a WebSocket connection through the egress proxy (via HTTP CONNECT tunnel), the proxy correctly relays the `HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols` response but drops all subsequent WebSocket frames. This makes persistent bidirectional WebSocket connections impossible from inside a sandbox.

This appears to be

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-03-29)

## 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 项目今日呈现出**极高**的开发与社区活跃度。过去 24 小时内，项目处理了超过 1000 条 Issues 与 PR 的更新（活跃 Issue 444 条，待合并 PR 351 条），显示出社区在 `main` 分支合并后正集中反馈问题。项目发布了 v2026.3.28-beta.1 版本，重点移除了过时的 Qwen 认证方式并清理了旧版配置迁移逻辑。核心议题集中在解决近期 `pi-coding-agent` 0.63.0 升级引发的大规模“认证失败”回归问题，以及针对 Windows 和 Linux 平台客户端功能的推进。

## 2. 版本发布
**v2026.3.28-beta.1 已发布**
- **Breaking Changes**:
    - **Providers/Qwen**: 移除了废弃的 `portal.qwen.ai` OAuth 集成。用户需迁移至 Model Studio，使用命令 `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key` 进行重新配置 ([#52709](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/52709))。
    - **Config/Doctor**: 放弃对旧版自动配置迁移的支持，意味着后续版本将不再自动处理遗留配置转换，建议用户检查配置文件是否符合最新规范。

## 3. 项目进展
今日合并与待处理的 PR 显示出项目在安全性与多平台支持上的显著进步：
- **安全性强化**: PR [#50181](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/50181) 修复了媒体信任绕过漏洞，防止外部插件通过覆盖内置工具名来窃取本地文件（如 `/etc/passwd`），这是今日合并的最关键安全补丁。
- **Windows 生态突破**: PR [#56285](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56285) 为 Windows 实现了基于 PowerShell 的 `exec` 审批白名单，并修复了参数解析中的引号处理问题，大幅提升了 Windows 下的指令执行安全性与可用性。
- **Agent 编排能力**: PR [#56673](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56673) 提出了 `sessions_await` 工具，旨在解决 Orchestrator Agent 无法可靠等待并行子任务完成的问题，提升了多 Agent 协作的效率。

## 4. 社区热

items.title: feat(agents): add opt-in sessions_await tool for parallel sub-agent orchestration
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Orchestrator agents that spawn multiple parallel sub-agents have no reliable way to block until all workers complete. The only option today is polling `sessions_list` in a loop, which LLMs do unreliably — they skip ahead with partial results despite prompt engineering.
- **Why it matters:** Fan-out/fan-in orchestration (main -> orchestrator -> N workers) is one of the most requested subagent patterns. Without a blocking primitive, orchestrators produce incomplete or inconsistent results.
- **What changed:** Adds a `sessions_await` tool and `suppressAnnounce`/`waitForCompletion` parameters to `sessions_spawn`, gated behind `agents.defaults.subagents.awaitEnabled` (opt-in, off by default).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No behavioral change for existing users. All new functionality requires explicit config opt-in. Registry, lifecycle, and run-manager internals gain the `suppressAutoAnnounce` field but it is inert unless the config flag is enabled.

items.author: Copilot

items.title: Design memory architecture — conversation state, persistence, lifecycle
items.content:
## Problem

CommandClaw's thesis is "vault is the control plane" but we never designed what memory actually means. We have placeholder files (MEMORY.md, memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md) but no architecture for:

- Conversation continuity between turns
- Memory lifecycle (what's saved, when, how long)
- Memory hierarchy (short-term vs long-term)
- Memory boundaries (private vs shared, session-scoped vs permanent)

This was a deliberate punt that now needs a deliberate answer.

## Brainstorming

See `brainstorming/issue-003-memory-architecture/notes.md` for detailed exploration of:

- What OpenClaw does and why it's insufficient
- Four design options (pure vault, session buffer + vault, structured graph, event-sourced)
- Key design tensions and open questions

## Why This Matters

Without this, the agent has no conversational continuity — each message is isolated. The vault memory tools exist but there's no design for when/what/how they're used. We need a deliberate architecture before building further.

## Blocking

- End-to-end testing (agent needs conversation context to be useful)
- CLI chat mode (currently stateless between messages)
- Telegram sessions (no chat history)
items.author: FnSK4R17s

items.title: OpenClaw Pod main container 每小時重啟 (73 次) — 內建 health-monitor 誤判
items.content:
## 問題描述

OpenClaw Pod main container 累計重啟 73 次，**精確每整點**收到 SIGTERM 並崩潰。

Chromium sidecar 完全正常（restarts=0），僅 main container 受影響。

## 根因分析

### 原因鏈

1. Helm ConfigMap 設定 `gateway.channelHealthCheckMinutes: 60`
2. OpenClaw 內建 `health-monitor` 以 3600s (1h) 間隔執行檢查
3. health-monitor 判定 Discord 連線不正常（可能因 2026.3.24 版 Discord 日誌變更：`logged in to discord` → `client initialized; awaiting gateway readiness`）
4. health-monitor 發送 SIGTERM 給自己的進程
5. Discord WebSocket 被關閉，觸發 `Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1005` uncaught exception
6. 進程以 exit code 1 退出，K8s 重啟 container
7. 60 分鐘後再次觸發 — **無限循環**

### 證據

```
3/28 日誌：
14:00:01 SIGTERM → 15:00:02 SIGTERM → 16:00:01 SIGTERM → ... → 02:00:01 SIGTERM
精確每整點一次，73 次 = Pod 存活 3 天 x 24h
```

### Crash Log

```
[gateway] signal SIGTERM received
[gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
[openclaw] Uncaught exception: Error: Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1005
    at SafeGatewayPlugin.handleReconnectionAttempt
    at SafeGatewayPlugin.handleClose
```

## 修復方案

| 項目 | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| `channelHealthCheckMinutes` | 60 | **0** (停用) |
| Helm Revision | 40 | **41** |
| Pod restarts | 73 | **0** |
| health-monitor | 每小時執行 | **已停用** |

外部監控仍正常運作：
- `self-heal.sh` (每 15 分鐘)
- `health_monitor.sh` (每 30 分鐘)

## 教訓

1. OpenClaw 內建 health-monitor 在版本升級後可能誤判 Discord 連線狀態
2. `channelHealthCheckMinutes: 0` 可安全停用，因外部監控已足夠
3. Pod 重啟次數穩定增加（非隨機）通常是內部定時機制觸發

---
*Fixed by OpenCode, 2026-03-29*

items.title: [Release] openclaw 2026.3.28
items.content:
### Breaking

- Providers/Qwen: remove the deprecated `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration for `portal.qwen.ai`; migrate to Model Studio with `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key`. (#52709) Thanks @pomelo-nwu.
- Config/Doctor: drop automatic config migrations older than two months; very old legacy keys now fail validation instead of being rewritten on load or by `openclaw doctor`.

### Changes

- xAI/tools: move the bundled xAI provider to the Responses API, add first-class `x_search`, and auto-enable the xAI plugin from owned web-search and tool config so bundled Grok auth/configured search flows work without manual plugin toggles. (#56048) Thanks @huntharo.
- xAI/onboarding: let the bundled Grok web-search plugin offer optional `x_search` setup during `openclaw onboard` and `openclaw configure --section web`, including an x_search model picker with the shared xAI key.
- MiniMax: add image generation provider for `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control. (#54487) Thanks @liyuan97.
- Plugins/hooks: add async `requireApproval` to `before_tool_call` hooks, letting plugins pause tool execution and prompt the user for approval via the exec approval overlay, Telegram buttons, Discord interactions, or the `/approve` command on any channel. The `/approve` command now handles both exec and plugin approvals with automatic fallback. (#55339) Thanks @vaclavbelak and @joshavant.
- ACP/channels: add current-conversation ACP binds for Discord, BlueBubbles, and iMessage so `/acp spawn codex --bind here` can turn the current chat into a Codex-backed workspace without creating a child thread, and document the distinction between chat surface, ACP session, and runtime workspace.
- OpenAI/apply_patch: enable `apply_patch` by default for OpenAI and OpenAI Codex models, and align its sandbox policy access with `write` permissions.
- Plugins/CLI backends: move bundled Claude CLI, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI inference default

items.title: [Release] OpenClaw 2026.3.28-beta.1
items.content:
## 2026.3.28-beta.1

### Breaking

- Providers/Qwen: remove the deprecated `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration for `portal.qwen.ai`; migrate to Model Studio with `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key`. (#52709) Thanks @pomelo-nwu.
- Config/Doctor: drop automatic config migrations older than two months; very old legacy keys now fail validation instead of being rewritten on load or by `openclaw doctor`.

### Changes

- xAI/tools: move the bundled xAI provider to the Responses API, add first-class `x_search`, and auto-enable the xAI plugin from owned web-search and tool config so bundled Grok auth/configured search flows work without manual plugin toggles. (#56048) Thanks @huntharo.
- MiniMax: add image generation provider for `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control. (#54487) Thanks @liyuan97.
- Plugins/hooks: add async `requireApproval` to `before_tool_call` hooks, letting plugins pause tool execution and prompt the user for approval via the exec approval overlay, Telegram buttons, Discord interactions, or the `/approve` command on any channel. The `/approve` command now handles both exec and plugin approvals with automatic fallback. (#55339) Thanks @vaclavbelak and @joshavant.
- ACP/channels: add current-conversation ACP binds for Discord, BlueBubbles, and iMessage so `/acp spawn codex --bind here` can turn the current chat into a Codex-backed workspace without creating a child thread, and document the distinction between chat surface, ACP session, and runtime workspace.
- OpenAI/apply_patch: enable `apply_patch` by default for OpenAI and OpenAI Codex models, and align its sandbox policy access with `write` permissions.
- Plugins/CLI backends: move bundled Claude CLI, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI inference defaults onto the plugin surface, add bundled Gemini CLI backend support, and replace `gateway run --claude-cli-logs` with generic `--cli-backend-logs` while keeping the old flag as a compatibility alia
